---
title: "About This Tutorial"
subtitle: "Machine Learning in Python — from scratch, for everyone"
date: last-modified
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
number-sections: false
---

## What Is This?

**Machine Learning in Python** is a free, open-source tutorial that teaches every major ML algorithm by building it from scratch using only the Python standard library and the built-in `math` module — no NumPy, no scikit-learn, no black boxes.

The goal is to give you a genuine understanding of *how* these algorithms work, not just how to call a library function.

---

## Who Is It For?

This tutorial is designed for:

- Python programmers who want to understand ML from first principles
- Students taking an introductory ML course and looking for a practical companion
- Engineers who use ML libraries and want to demystify what's happening under the hood
- Anyone curious about the mathematics behind modern AI systems

**You need:** basic Python (variables, loops, functions, lists) and high-school algebra. No prior ML experience required.

---

## Structure

The tutorial is organized into three levels:

| Level | Chapters | Topics |
|-------|----------|--------|
| 🟢 Beginner | 00–05 | Prerequisites, Intro to ML, Linear Regression, Logistic Regression, KNN, Naive Bayes |
| 🟡 Intermediate | 06–08, 12–13 | Decision Trees, SVM, Ensemble Methods, K-Means, PCA |
| 🔴 Advanced | 09–11, 14 | Neural Networks, CNNs, RNNs, Reinforcement Learning |
| 📊 All Levels | 15 | Model Evaluation & Best Practices |

Each chapter includes:

- Intuitive explanations before any math
- Step-by-step derivations
- Full Python implementations from scratch
- Worked examples with real output
- Exercises to test your understanding

---

## Philosophy

> *"I hear and I forget. I see and I remember. I do and I understand."* — Confucius

Every algorithm in this tutorial is implemented without ML libraries. This means:

- No hidden behaviour — you can read every line
- Genuine understanding — you can modify and experiment
- Portable knowledge — the math translates to any language or framework

---

## License

This tutorial is released under the [MIT License](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).
You are free to use, copy, modify, and distribute it — with attribution.

---

## Contributing

Found a bug? Have an improvement? Open an issue or pull request on [GitHub](https://github.com/zia207/ML-python).

---

## Author

Built with [Quarto](https://quarto.org) and hosted on GitHub Pages.
